title: The Water Made Wine
subtitle: The wedding at Cana, where Christ honors marriage and turns water into wine

stillness: Be still, and grow quiet within.
It is fitting that where God is invited,
the wine of passing gladness should run dry.
Let that lesser wine fail in you now,
and wait for the wine He alone can give.
gospel: On the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee,
and the mother of Jesus was there.
And Jesus also was called, and his disciples, to the wedding.
And when the wine ran short,
the mother of Jesus said to him: They have no wine.
Jesus said to her: What is that to me and to thee, woman?
My hour is not yet come.
behold: Behold, therefore, the Lord Jesus among the others,
eating like any one of the common people,
and humbly seated in the lowest place,
For He would one day teach:
When thou art invited to a wedding, take the last place —
and He chose first to do what He would later teach.
Behold also the Lady, attentive and devout,
anxious that all be done rightly and in order.
Near the end of the feast she sees the wine failing,
and goes to her Son, and says: They have no wine.
She asks for nothing; she only names the need,
knowing that to one who loves, it is enough to name the need,
consider: Do not think it chance, that when the Lord came to the wedding the wine failed;
rather, it was so that an occasion for the miracle might be made.
Full of the Holy Spirit, she already foresaw what her Son would do.
From a most loving heart she wished to forestall the total failure,
lest shame and confusion appear upon the poor.
It is fitting, that where God is invited, the wine of temporal gladness should fail;
for the Saints take no delight in that wine,
which makes a man drunk unto forgetfulness of God,
and sets him aflame toward base desire.
And there is no doubt that Christ would never have entered among those who delight in such wine;
and so it is fitting that at the weddings of the Saints it should run dry.
He called her woman, not because her virginity was wronged,
but by the manner of speaking of her people, who called every woman so.
My hour is not yet come, that is: those present do not yet feel the lack of wine;
let them first feel it, for when they have known the need,
they will the more reckon as a gift the kindness they receive.
prayer: who on the third day — that is, in the time of grace —
came in the flesh to the wedding,
the joining of Thyself and Thy Church,
and didst turn water into wine,
showing that the things which in the old Scripture seemed carnal are spiritual:
Turn, I beseech Thee, my cold soul into the fervor of Thy love;
my tasteless soul, into the savor of Thy sweetness;
my unsteady, wavering soul, into the constancy of Thy strength and grace.
Change the water of all my snow-cold lukewarmness
into the wine of fruitful compunction,
that I, wretched as I am, may drink of it and be made drunk,
and at the last, of Thy mercy, vouchsafe to turn it into the wine of gladness. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Sciens quod amanti sufficit solum insinuare necessitatem, absque ulla petitione.
latinJewel.english: Knowing that, for one who loves, it is enough only to hint at the need, without any petition at all.
